sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_date DATE, location INET);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, location) VALUES (1, DATE '2021-12-31', inet '192.168.1.1');
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, location) VALUES (2, DATE '2022-01-15', inet '192.168.1.2');
SELECT id, sql_add(event_date, 1) AS new_date FROM test_dates WHERE location = inet '192.168.1.1';
SELECT id, sql_add((SELECT event_date FROM test_dates WHERE location = inet '192.168.1.2'), 3) AS future_date FROM test_dates;
SELECT id, sql_add(event_date, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates)) AS dynamic_date FROM test_dates GROUP BY id HAVING dynamic_date >= DATE '2022-02-01';
SELECT DISTINCT location, sql_add(event_date, 6) FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL AND location = inet '192.168.1.1';
SELECT id, sql_add(COALESCE(event_date, DATE '2000-01-01'), -1) FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NULL;
SELECT id, sql_add(event_date, 1) AS incremented_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_date >= DATE '2021-01-01' AND location IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE extra_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data_value VARCHAR(50), FOREIGN KEY (id) REFERENCES test_dates(id));
INSERT INTO extra_data (id, data_value) VALUES (1, 'Test Data A');
SELECT ed.id, ed.data_value, sql_add(td.event_date, 12) FROM extra_data ed JOIN test_dates td ON ed.id = td.id WHERE td.event_date <= DATE '2021-12-31';
SELECT ed.id, COUNT(*), sql_add(td.event_date, (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_dates)) FROM extra_data ed JOIN test_dates td ON ed.id = td.id GROUP BY ed.id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
UPDATE test_dates SET event_date = sql_add(event_date, 1) WHERE location = inet '192.168.1.1';
